prompt_text:
Analyze the following java classes for violations of the Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP).

For each class you are given:
- Its full source body
- Its base class bodies (where available in the repo)
- The file's imports
- Short headers of sibling classes in the same file

Use the inheritance and import context to reason about dependencies and substitutability.

## Principle Definition
High-level modules should not depend on low-level modules; both should depend on abstractions. Abstractions ...
raw_response:
{
  "findings": [
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/JsonParserBase.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonParserBase",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 74,
      "line_end": 80,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Abstract base class depends on a concrete context implementation.",
      "reasoning": "The abstract class JsonParserBase directly instantiates and depends on the concrete class JsonReadContext via the call to JsonReadContext.createRootContext(dups). This violates DIP as the high-level abstraction (JsonParserBase) depends on a low-level implementation detail (JsonReadContext) instead of an abstraction."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/json/JsonGeneratorBase.java",
      "entity_name": "JsonGeneratorBase",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 164,
      "line_end": 181,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Abstract base class depends on a concrete context implementation.",
      "reasoning": "Similar to the parser base, JsonGeneratorBase directly instantiates and depends on the concrete class JsonWriteContext. Abstractions should not depend on details; the management of the write context should be abstracted or injected."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/TokenStreamFactory.java",
      "entity_name": "TokenStreamFactory",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 226,
      "line_end": 237,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Factory class hard-codes dependency on concrete recycler pools.",
      "reasoning": "The constructor of TokenStreamFactory directly calls JsonRecyclerPools.defaultPool(), coupling the high-level factory abstraction to a specific concrete implementation of buffer recycling. This makes it difficult to substitute different recycling strategies without modifying the factory."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/TokenStreamFactory.java",
      "entity_name": "TokenStreamFactory._createContext",
      "entity_type": "method",
      "line_start": 1277,
      "line_end": 1301,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "high",
      "description": "Factory method directly instantiates a concrete I/O context.",
      "reasoning": "The method _createContext directly instantiates the concrete class IOContext using the 'new' keyword. This violates DIP because the high-level factory module depends on the low-level implementation details of the I/O context rather than an abstraction or a provider."
    },
    {
      "file_path": "src/main/java/tools/jackson/core/TokenStreamFactory.java",
      "entity_name": "TokenStreamFactory",
      "entity_type": "class",
      "line_start": 55,
      "line_end": 55,
      "principle": "DIP",
      "severity": "medium",
      "description": "Static constant instantiates a concrete implementation of an interface.",
      "reasoning": "The EMPTY_WRITE_CONTEXT constant is initialized by directly instantiating ObjectWriteContext.Base. This couples the abstract TokenStreamFactory to a specific default implementation of the write context."
    }
  ]
}

findings.description: Abstract base class depends on a concrete context implementation.
findings.reasoning: The abstract class JsonParserBase directly instantiates and depends on the concrete class JsonReadContext via the call to JsonReadContext.createRootContext(dups). This violates DIP as the high-level abstraction (JsonParserBase) depends on a low-level implementation detail (JsonReadContext) instead of an abstraction.

findings.description: Abstract base class depends on a concrete context implementation.
findings.reasoning: Similar to the parser base, JsonGeneratorBase directly instantiates and depends on the concrete class JsonWriteContext. Abstractions should not depend on details; the management of the write context should be abstracted or injected.

findings.description: Factory class hard-codes dependency on concrete recycler pools.
findings.reasoning: The constructor of TokenStreamFactory directly calls JsonRecyclerPools.defaultPool(), coupling the high-level factory abstraction to a specific concrete implementation of buffer recycling. This makes it difficult to substitute different recycling strategies without modifying the factory.

findings.description: Factory method directly instantiates a concrete I/O context.
findings.reasoning: The method _createContext directly instantiates the concrete class IOContext using the 'new' keyword. This violates DIP because the high-level factory module depends on the low-level implementation details of the I/O context rather than an abstraction or a provider.

findings.description: Static constant instantiates a concrete implementation of an interface.
findings.reasoning: The EMPTY_WRITE_CONTEXT constant is initialized by directly instantiating ObjectWriteContext.Base. This couples the abstract TokenStreamFactory to a specific default implementation of the write context.
